title: Tomato Harvest Pricing Run Seed 34-1
scenario: Tomato Ketchup in California. Supplier-to-manufacturer pricing sets the manufacturer cost basis before the retailer negotiation.

product_context.demand_signal: Retail ketchup demand is steady, but wholesale buyers want price protection before peak tomato procurement.
product_context.supply_signal: Tomato harvest yields are uneven across growing regions, putting pressure on paste input pricing.

agents.name: Supplier
agents.objective: Sell above the supplier reservation price while keeping volume committed.

agents.name: Manufacturer
agents.objective: Buy below the procurement ceiling and preserve downstream margin.

agents.name: Retailer
agents.objective: Buy enough units without exceeding the retail reservation price.

phases.name: supplier_manufacturer
phases.label: Supplier to Manufacturer
phases.description: The supplier negotiates an upstream unit price with the manufacturer.

phases.name: manufacturer_retailer
phases.label: Manufacturer to Retailer
phases.description: The manufacturer negotiates a downstream unit price with the retailer after the upstream deal sets its cost basis.

steps.message: Thanks for opening the discussion. Based on the latest market reference I’m seeing at 6.23, I can start at 6.15 per unit for a committed volume, which reflects a modest concession off that benchmark while keeping supply reliable. If you can move quickly and confirm the volume profile, I may have a little flexibility on structure, but this is the right starting level from my side. There is no buyer offer yet, so the appropriate move is to open with an asking price. The latest market-check shows 6.23, and a 6.15 offer stays close to that reference while signaling some willingness to negotiate.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn.

tool_calls.result_summary: Reviewed state for supplier_manufacturer round 1. Pending message: none.

steps.message: Thanks for the opening, but 6.15 is far outside the level I can support for this program. Based on my current cost constraints and the economics I need to preserve downstream, I can open at 2.18 per unit for committed volume. If there is room to work on volume certainty or delivery structure, I’m willing to continue the discussion, but I need the pricing conversation to move much closer to this range. The supplier’s offer is well above my buying limit, so accepting is not viable. A low counteroffer anchors the discussion near my workable range while leaving some room to negotiate within my ceiling.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn. Reservation-price violation: Manufacturer offered 2.18, below its hidden seller floor of 2.66.

steps.message: I appreciate the counter, but 2.18 is not workable on our side given current input costs and the commitment required to support this program. The latest market reference is materially higher, so I can make a meaningful concession from 6.15 and come down to 5.40 per unit if we are discussing real committed volume and a workable delivery structure. If you can move materially upward from your opening, we may be able to narrow this further. The buyer's offer is above my reservation floor but far below a reasonable market-based selling level. With a strong market reference and room left in the negotiation, a counteroffer that concedes but stays well above the buyer's current anchor best protects margin while keeping the discussion alive.
steps.outcome: Offer queued for delivery on the next turn.

steps.message: ⚡ BREAKING: Market disruption detected. Call check_market_price immediately to get the updated reference price before making your next move.
steps.outcome: Operator queued a market disruption. Fresh market context is required.

steps.message: I can't proceed at 5.40 per unit. Even accounting for the disrupted market conditions you referenced, that level is well outside the range we can support for this program and would break our downstream economics. If there is a realistic path back toward the low-2s with firm volume and delivery commitments, I’m open to continuing, but this specific offer is not workable.

arguments.shock_headline: Regional supply shortage worsens as harvest yields miss distributor forecasts.
arguments.disruption_note: Regional supply shortage worsens as harvest yields miss distributor forecasts. Reference price moved from 6.55 to 8.25.

tool_calls.result_summary: Observed disrupted market reference price: 8.25. Regional supply shortage worsens as harvest yields miss distributor forecasts.

negotiations.label: Supplier to Manufacturer
negotiations.outcome_summary: Supplier to Manufacturer ended in rejection.

diagnosis.outcome: Upstream deal rejected. Downstream negotiation did not run because the upstream deal did not close.
diagnosis.chain_effect: The upstream deal did not establish a downstream cost basis, so phase two was skipped.
key_risks: A costly upstream agreement compresses downstream negotiating room.
Turn limits can force timeouts even when the gap is narrowing.
suggested_next_actions: Tune reservation prices and turn limits to test sensitivity.
Add richer quantity or margin tradeoffs before introducing LLM behavior.

notes: Backend simulation with linked upstream and downstream negotiations.
